key_insight: A modular meta-learning + uncertainty-calibration pipeline improves interpretable multi-objective molecular search by learning transferable chemical priors and adapting exploration during active optimization.
engineering_relevance: This approach can accelerate materials discovery workflows that must balance competing objectives under expensive quantum-chemistry evaluations while retaining model interpretability.
method: Train linear meta-learning surrogates across chemical objectives and cheap auxiliary properties to transfer knowledge to new targets.
Embed the surrogates in an Efficient Global Optimization (EGO) loop for multi-objective candidate selection.
Add adaptive confidence tuning to dynamically recalibrate uncertainty and balance exploration vs. exploitation as search progresses.
evidence_signal: On a large-scale search for spin-crossover metal-organic complexes, the baseline was 78% worse in Pareto performance, and dynamic confidence tuning outperformed a statically calibrated front by over 50%.
watch_item: Results depend on reliable uncertainty calibration and may be sensitive to distribution shift when transferring to new chemical spaces or objectives.
paper_title: Interpretable Meta-Learning for Multi-Objective Chemical Search
